summary_one_line: ICC profile data colorspace tag at offsets 0x10-0x16 must spell 'Lab Lab ' (an 8-bit RGB-compatible formatter) rather than 'RGB XYZ '.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The profile colorspace/PCS signature bytes at input offsets 0x10-0x16 must encode a value whose derived InputFormat passes _cmsFormatterIs8bit. Winners carry the 4-byte ASCII run 'Lab ' (4c 61 62 20) at both 0x10 and 0x14; losers carry 'RGB ' (52 47 42 20) and 'XYZ ' (58 59 5a 20). The blocker on line 1676 demands an 8-bit InputFormat, which only the winner's colorspace fields produce.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds hold 4c 61 62 (Lab) at offsets 0x10-0x12 and 0x14-0x16 (BYTE DIFF shows 0x10=4c, 0x11=61, 0x12=62, 0x14=4c, 0x15=61, 0x16=62), and the cmplog/vpc winners discovered the branch in 1.3-2.3h, resolving it in 10/10 trials with avg hitcount 53-71 on the true side.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds hold 52 47 42 (RGB) at 0x10-0x12 and 58 59 5a (XYZ) at 0x14-0x16 (BYTE DIFF DIFFER rows), so InputFormat is not 8-bit; line 1676 takes the false (return FALSE) side in all 10 trials, blocked 24.00h with hitcount 0 on the true side.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace intercepts the integer/string CMPs inside _cmsFormatterIs8bit's format derivation, logs the expected 8-bit-formatter operand, finds the colorspace byte run in the input and substitutes the matching value at offsets 0x10-0x16. naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and never planted the exact colorspace bytes that yield an 8-bit InputFormat.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a guard requiring an 8-bit input formatter at cmsopt.c:1676.
Both decisive pairs differ by exactly the I2S technique.
The second decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog over value_profile with delta I2S.
Winner seeds carry 'Lab ' at offset 0x10 while losers carry 'RGB '.
Winner seeds carry 'Lab' second field at 0x14 while losers carry 'XYZ'.
A winner-resolving cmplog seed contains the 'Lab Lab ' colorspace run in its header.
A loser-blocking seed contains the 'RGB XYZ ' colorspace run in its header.
Winners resolve the branch in 10/10 trials with positive true-side hitcount; losers block all 10 with zero true-side hits.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1676  T=20 F=0  T=0 F=20  if (!_cmsFormatterIs8bit(*InputFormat)) return FALSE;  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a logged CMP operand at input offsets matching the other operand.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 2228 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 0aedfccd0898c3ff` and observed an I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -, ancestor 5441efb6193a1a03) at depth 16 of the 17-level chain; tool reported '1 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 16', a lower-bound I2S contribution signal exclusive to cmplog/vpc.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on an 8-bit-formatter colorspace tag where naive/value_profile resolve the branch as fast as cmplog/vpc would refute the I2S attribution, showing the colorspace bytes are reachable by plain havoc/CMP_MAP without input-to-state substitution.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact CMP chain inside _cmsFormatterIs8bit that derives the 8-bit InputFormat from the colorspace tag is inferred from the byte diff and source, not directly observed as a logged I2S operand substitution at offsets 0x10-0x16.
